images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of the Implicit Preference Alignment (IPA) framework for enhancing hand generation quality. IPA eliminates the necessity for bad samples inherent in standard preference optimization frameworks (e.g., direct preference optimization), alleviating the burden for preference annotation. We have also theoretically proved in Sec.4.2that IPA inherently performs implicit reward maximization.

Figure 2:Visual comparisons of different methods.
Existing methods often suffer from malformed or collapsed hand appearances.
In contrast, our approach yields hands with sharp edges and distinct finger separation, closely matching the Ground Truth.
Complete comparisons can be found in Fig.7.
Figure 3:Visual results of ablation study for key components.
Figure 4:Ablation study on differentОІ\beta. We can observe that the optimal performance is achieved whenОІ=600\beta=600.

Figure 6:Visual results for differentОІ\beta. ForОІ=200\beta=200, the model produces anatomically impossible artifacts (i.e., an extraneous third hand). WhenОІ=2000\beta=2000, the generated hands suffer from blurry artifacts and distortions. ForОІ=600\beta=600, the generated hands exhibit clear structures and are devoid of anatomically impossible artifacts.
Figure 7:Complete visual comparisons of different methods for the case of Fig.2.
Figure 8:More visual comparisons of different methods.
Figure 9:More showcases of human image animation generated by our method.
Figure 10:More showcases of human image animation generated by our method.
